topic: Someone receives an invitation to their high school reunion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The thick, cream-colored envelope felt heavy in Maya’s hand, the embossed seal feeling almost accusatory. A high school reunion. It was a concept she hadn't actively sought out, and the sheer formality of the invitation made her stomach clench. She traced the elegant script detailing the date and location, the familiar names swimming uncomfortably in her mind. Every name felt like a potential trap. When she finally set the letter down on the worn wooden table, the silence of her apartment seemed amplified, pressing in on her ears. She kept glancing at the window, half-expecting someone to be watching from the street below. A tremor ran through her fingers as she reread the RSVP details, the ink blurring slightly under the pressure of her rising anxiety.

[story 2]

Liam stared at the invitation as if it were a foreign object dropped into his carefully organized workspace. The glossy paper seemed too bright, the cheerful font mocking the knot tightening in his chest. He folded it meticulously, smoothing out every crease, a nervous habit he couldn't shake. He kept running a hand over the smooth surface, his knuckles whitening. He imagined the gathering—the forced smiles, the superficial catch-ups—and a cold slickness began to creep up his neck. Why was he even receiving this? He looked around his apartment, suddenly finding the familiar furniture alien and too loud. He needed to throw the invitation away, to erase the possibility, but his hand remained frozen above the discard pile.

[story 3]

"A celebration of shared history," the invitation read. Sarah read the words aloud to herself, her voice sounding brittle even to her own ears. She folded the paper in half repeatedly, the edges crisping under her anxious grip. Her breath hitched, shallow and quick. She found herself pacing the length of her living room, the floorboards groaning beneath her bare feet. Each step felt heavy, disconnected. She kept touching her collarbone, a small, repetitive gesture, trying to anchor herself against a strange, internal vibration that made the air feel thin. She couldn't bring herself to look at the photo of the venue; the setting felt suddenly too exposed.
